2604.06794.title: GCoT-Decoding: Unlocking Deep Reasoning Paths for Universal Question Answering
2604.06794.paper_summary: Chain-of-Thought reasoning can enhance large language models, but it requires manually designed prompts to guide the model. Recently proposed CoT-decoding enables the model to generate CoT-style reasoning paths without prompts, but it is only applicable to problems with fixed answer sets. To address this limitation, we propose a general decoding strategy GCoT-decoding that extends applicability to a broader range of question-answering tasks. GCoT-decoding employs a two-stage branching method combining Fibonacci sampling and heuristic error backtracking to generate candidate decoding paths. It then splits each path into a reasoning span and an answer span to accurately compute path confidence, and finally aggregates semantically similar paths to identify a consensus answer, replacing traditional majority voting. We conduct extensive experiments on six datasets covering both fixed and free QA tasks. Our method not only maintains strong performance on fixed QA but also achieves significant improvements on free QA, demonstrating its generality.
2604.06794.paper_summary_zh: Chain-of-Thought 推理可以增強大型語言模型，但它需要手動設計的提示來指導模型。最近提出的 CoT-decoding 使模型能夠在沒有提示的情況下生成 CoT 風格的推理路徑，但僅適用於具有固定答案集的問題。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了一種通用解碼策略 GCoT-decoding，擴展了其在更廣泛的問答任務中的適用性。GCoT-decoding 採用結合斐波那契抽樣和啟發式錯誤回溯的兩階段分支方法來生成候選解碼路徑。然後，它將每條路徑拆分為推理範圍和答案範圍，以準確計算路徑置信度，並最終聚合語義相似的路徑以識別共識答案，取代傳統的多數投票。我們在六個數據集上進行了廣泛的實驗，涵蓋了固定和自由的問答任務。我們的方法不僅在固定問答上保持強勁的表現，還在自由問答上取得了顯著的改進，展示了其通用性。
2604.06794.author: Guanran Luo et.al.
2604.06794.authors: Guanran Luo, Wentao Qiu, Zhongquan Jian, Meihong Wang, Qingqiang Wu
